translations.en: a jolly, red-faced innkeeper.
translations.zh: 一位臉色紅潤、笑容可掬的客棧老闆。

translations.en: "Vell, hello
again
dere
, mine friend
! Velcome to the Varrior's Stead!"
"Vat can I be doing for thee this $T?"
"Mine name ist Martin, but my friends call me '@Dutch.'"
translations.zh: 「吾的名字是馬丁，但朋友們叫吾『@荷蘭人』。」

translations.en: "Ja, dat's mine name!"
"I run dis @inn."
"Room und board ist 5 gold per night per person."
translations.zh: 「住宿含膳食，每人每晚 5 枚金幣。」

translations.en: "Interested?"
"Haf a good journey!" he calls as you leave.
translations.zh: 「祝旅途愉快！」他在汝離去時呼喊道。

translations.en: "I speak your language not so good. Vat is meaning '$Z'?"
translations.zh: 「吾說汝們的語言不太好。『$Z』是什麼意思？」

translations.en: "Vell, perhaps some other time, ja?"
"Ach, I'm sorry, but I must haf my #0 gold."
translations.zh: 「唉，抱歉，但吾必須收取 #0 枚金幣。」

translations.en: "Come back ven fortune makes you rich, ah?"
He shows you to your room, and you drift off into a well-earned sleep.
translations.zh: 他領汝到房間，汝沉入了當之無愧的好眠。

translations.en: The next morning, Martin serves you a huge, tasty breakfast.
translations.zh: 翌日清晨，馬丁端上一頓豐盛可口的早餐。

translations.en: "A good breakfast is the beginning of a good day!" he cries.
translations.zh: 「美好的一天從豐盛的早餐開始！」他高喊道。

translator_notes: 馬丁（Dutch）是個帶荷蘭腔的客棧老闆，把 W 說成 V（Vell/Vat/Velcome）。中文無法直接模擬此音，但保留其熱情豪爽的個性。Dutch 譯為「荷蘭人」以保留趣味。Martin 已在 glossary。
